def: s.f. (local) lit. 'Brought under the yoke'; land actually in possession (as opp. to that to which a man is entitled by descent from a common ancestor).
s.m. A sin, crime, fault, offence, transgression, misdemeanour
subs: s.m. Minor or petty offence (such as is bailable); — jurm-ě-ḵẖilāf-wāẓʻ-ě-fitrī, s.m. An unnatural offence, sodomy
s.m. A serious crime, felony; a capital crime
To deny an offence, to plead not guilty
s.m. A crime deserving of death, a capital crime
v.n. To commit or perpetrate a crime
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian جُرْم (jurm), from Arabic جُرْم (jurm).

def: s.m. Penalty, forfeit, fine
subs: jurmāna denā, v.n. To pay a penalty or fine
v.t. To impose a penalty, inflict or levy a fine, to fine, mulct

def: v.n. To be obtained, &c. = juṛnā, q.v.

ety: fr. the trans. joṛnā, or i.q. juṭnā, q.v.
def: v.n. To be joined, be attached; to join, unite; to copulate; to come to hand, be got, be had. be obtained or procured.
v.n. (Braj.) = jurnā.
s.m. = jurra, q.v.

ety: for A. جرة jurrat, fr. جرأ; cf. jarī
def: s.m. (lit. 'Bold, brave; active, swift'); a falcon (the male of the bāz), Falco saker.
s.m. A stocking, sock; leggings.
s.m. = jurra, q.v.
s.f. (prov.) = jorū, q.v.

ety: juṛwā˚, rt. of juṛwā-nā+āʼī = Prk. एअव्वअडआ = S. (इ)तव्य+ता+इका
def: s.f. The state of being joined or attached, adhesion.
s.m. A twin, twins; a pair (cf. joṛwān).
v.t. = juṛānā, q.v.
v.t. = jurānā, q.v.
s.f. = juṛāṭ; juṛwāʼī, q.v.
s.m. Broth; pease-soup, pease-porridge; the water in which pulse has been boiled.

ety: wh. is prop. pl. of jasīm
def: adj. Great, large, big, bulky; big-bodied, corpulent.
s.f. Corpulence ( = jasāmat).
s.m. = jūs, q.v.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian جوش (jōš).

def: s.m. The water of boiled pulse, pease soup, porridge, &c.
s.m. The crumbs, remnants or leavings of a meal ( = jhūṭhā).
part. Served, obliged; worshipped, gratified by service.
s.m. The body, the corporeal form or figure.

ety: v.n. of justan, Pehl. zāyītan; Zend jad; S. gad
def: s.f. Searching; search
subs: just-o-jū, s.f. Searching, seeking; search, inquiry, quest, scrutiny, examination, investigation
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian جست.

def: adj. Strong, sharp, pungent; harsh, violent, passionate; clear.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian جست.

def: s.m. Power, strength.

ety: perf. part. of justan; see just
def: part. adj. Searched for, sought.

ety: Prk. जुत्तो or जुत्तं; S. युक्तः or युक्तं, rt. युज्
def: adj. Possessed of virtue, virtuous.
s.f. Brilliancy, &c. = jot or joti, q.v.
s.m. A shoe, a slipper, &c. = jūtā, q.v. (used chiefly in comp.)
subs: adj. & s.m. Beaten with shoes; mean, vile, infamous; — one who has been beaten with slippers.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀚𑁄𑀢𑁆𑀢 (jotta), from Sanskrit योक्त्र (yóktra, “rope, thong, halter”), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *yáwktram, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *yáwktram, from Proto-Indo-European *yéwg-tro-m, from *yewg- (“to yoke”).

def: s.m. The matted hair of Śiva; the twisted or matted hair of an ascetic.
s.m. The rope which connects the irrigating basket with the handle.

ety: Prk. जुत्तअं; S. युक्तकं, rt. युज्
def: s.m. A pair of shoes; a shoe, a slipper
subs: v.n. To raise a shoe (in order to beat), to be ready to apply the shoe
jūtā barasnā, v.n. = jūtā ćalnā, q.v.
s.f. jūtam-jūtā, s.m. Mutual application of shoes, a fight with shoes; a scuffle, fight
s.m. One kept in submission or rendered tractable by the application, or through dread of the shoe
jūtā mārnā (-ko), To apply the shoe (to), to give (one) a shoe-beating.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀚𑀼𑀢𑁆𑀢 (jutta, “joined”), from Sanskrit युक्त (yukta, “joined, yoked”) or युक्तक (yuktaka, “pair”).

def: adj. Arable ( = jutāʼū); — s.f. Ploughing, tillage, cultivation.
s.m. (f.?) A braid of hair, any knot or fillet of hair; the matted hair of Śiva or of ascetics.
adj. & s.m. = jūt-ḵẖor, and jūtī-ḵẖor.
v.t. To cause to be joined; to cause to be yoked; to conglutinate; to yoke; — v.n. To be joined; to be yoked; to be tilled ( = jutnā).
v.t. To unite closely together, to combine; to conglutinate, congeal, form into a concrete mass.
s.m. Ploughed or cultivated land; — a ploughman ( = jotār).

ety: rt. of jutā-nā+āʼū = Prk. एअव्वं = S. (इ)तव्यं
def: adj. Fit for cultivation; arable.

ety: Prk. जुत्तं or जुट्टं; S. युक्तं
def: s.m. A match, pair ( = joṛā)
subs: s.m. Two pieces coming together in the game of ćausar.

def: s.m. = jāṭh, q.v.
s.m. A body, band, company; a flock, herd ( = jathā)
subs: adj. Corporate, joint, possessed in common.

def: s.m. Leavings of food, &c. = jhūṭ, &c., q.v.
s.m. Leavings of food, &c. = jhūṭ, &c., q.v.
s.f. Land which bears two harvests during the year ( = jhūṭhan, q.v.).
s.f. = jūṭh = jhūṭ, q.v.
s.f. = jūthālī, q.v.
s.f. A kind of jasmine, Jasminum auriculatum.
adj. See s.v. jūth.

ety: Prk. जुत्तिआ; S. युक्त+इका; see jūtā
def: s.f. A shoe, a slipper
subs: v.n. One shoe to come on top of the other (superstitiously regarded as a sign of an impending journey)
(-ko), 'To strike or beat (one or a thing) against the shoe'; to spurn, disdain, despise, contemn
s.f. A fight with shoes; a general scuffle
jūtī-ḵẖora, adj. & s.m. Beaten with slippers; disgraced; mean, vile, infamous
adv. It concerns my shoes; my shoes may care
jūtī-mār, s.f. The act of beating with slippers
(-kī), 'To take up the shoes' (as a duty); to do menial service, to wait upon
To slink away or run off with (his) shoes under his arm
'To walk about making the shoes creak'; to walk about doing nothing
(-kī), 'To arrange or dispose the shoes;' to perform menial service; to pay respect or homage (to); to cringe (to)
v.n. To suffer a shoe-beating; to suffer indignity or humiliation; to remain in an abject state of submission
(-par), To give (one) a shoe-beating; to treat contemptuously, to disdain; to revile, taunt
(-kī), 'To have dāl put in his shoes'; to have a domestic quarrel.

def: s.f. = jūtiyāṅ, q.v.
s.m. A class of hereditary watchmen located in the tract under the Siwālik range of hills.
s.m. = jūtiyāl, q.v.
s.f. Land which bears two harvests during the year ( = jūthālī).

ety: jūtī, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आ caus. increment
def: v.t. To beat with a shoe or slipper (deemed an act of great degradation).

ety: juṭ˚ = Prk. जुट्ट(इ) or जुट्टे(इ) fr. जुट्ट = S. युक्त p.p.p. of rt. युज्
def: v.n. To unite, join, cohere; to close (as an open wound); to close (with, -se), engage in close fight; to adhere, stick; to be stitched; to copulate (with); to stick fast; to stay, wait; to wait (for), expect.
v.n. To be yoked (as an ox, &c., to a plough or cart), to be attached (to); to be ploughed or tilled; to labour, toil, drudge
s.f. A couple; — two rupees; — a bundle of tobacco leaves.
v.t. To cause to be joined, to cause to be yoked; to have (a field, &c.) ploughed or tilled (by).
s.m. (prov.), A yoke ( = jūʼā, q.v.).
adj. & s.m. Young, juvenile; — a young man, a youth
subs: s.m. 'A young king'; an heir-apparent associated with the reigning sovereign in the government, a crown prince
s.m. The dignity of heir-apparent and associate on the throne.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀚𑀽𑀤 (jūda), from Sanskrit द्यूत (dyūta, “gambling”).

def: s.m. (prov.) = jūʼāṛ = juʼāṭ q.v.
s.m.(?) A sack, bag; half of a (horse) load; the human body; guile, deceit, fraud ( = jul); anything open, expanded, or wide.

ety: Prk. जुवाणो; S. युवनः; — Old P. juān; Zend yavan; S. yuvan
def: s.m.f. Young, youthful, in the prime of manhood or womanhood; vigorous; blooming; — a young man or woman; a youth; an adult; a man, an able-bodied man; a soldier, warrior
subs: s.m. A proper or fine youth
adj. & s.m. Manly, brave, gallant, spirited, bold, intrepid; magnanimous, generous; — a spirited or brave fellow, a fine young fellow, a hero; a generous or large-hearted man
s.f. Manliness, bravery, courage, spirit, gallantry, intrepidity; magnanimity, generosity
s.m.f. One who has just attained to manhood or womanhood.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian جَوَان (jawān). Displaced جُْوان (juvān).

def: s.m. (prov.) = jūʼāṭ, q.v.
s.m. (prov.) = jūʼāṭ, q.v.
s.m. Flood-tide, flood

ety: S. यव+प्रकार or आकार; cf. also junhār
def: s.f. Indian millet, Andropogon sorghum, or Holcus sorghum; (local) maize, Indian corn.
s.m. Indian corn, maize; large millet; barley, &c.
s.m. A yoke or pair of oxen (engaged in work); as much land as can be ploughed by a pair of bullocks; (local) the area ploughed in half a day.
s.m. See s.v. juvā.
s.m. A gambler.
s.f. dim. of juwārā and juʼār, qq.v.
adj. & s.f. Youthful, young; — a young woman, a damsel.
s.m. A large wooden or stone mortar (for pounding grain); an oil-press; a sugar-mill.
ex: اور جو بات نہیں ماننی ہوتی اس کے لیے کوئی جواز پیش کر دیتے ہیں دراصل یہ جواز جھوٹ پر مبنی ہوتا ہے۔
aur jo bāt nahī̃ mānnī hotī us ke lie koī javāz peś kar dete ha͠i dar asl ye javāz jhūṭ par mabnī hotā hai.
And he gives a justification for whatever he does not wish to accept, but in actual fact that justification is based on lies.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian جَوَاز (jawāz), from Arabic جَوَاز (jawāz).

def: s.m. (prov.) = jūʼārī, q.v.
s.m. Part, portion; particle; component part, ingredient; part or section of a book (consisting of eight leaves); a printer's sheet; a quire (of paper); a metrical foot (pl. ajzā)
subs: s.f. Stitching the parts of a book together, book-binding
s.m. A cloth in which books are wrapped up, a satchel: a port-folio
adj. Penetrating, sagacious; frugal, thrifty, economical; penurious
s.m. An instrument used for keeping a book open when reading or writing; — a port-folio
s.m. An undivisible atom, anything too small to be divided, a monad
s.m. adj. & adv. Part and all, the whole; particulars; — great and small (matters), all; — totally, in toto, entirely; collectively.

ety: contrac. of judaz, i.e. juda+az, qq.v.
def: prep. & adv. Besides, except; — ba-juz, prep. With the exception (of), excepting.
s.m. A species of leprosy (so called because it causes the flesh to fall off, or because the fingers or toes drop off)
s.m. A leper.
adj. & s.f. = juzwī, q.v.
subs: s.f. Part and all; wholeness, totality, integrity.

def: s.f.pl. (of juzʼī), Small parts, particulars, minutiæ.
s.m. = juz, q.v.
adj. Relating to a part or portion, pantial, in part, few; particular; trivial, petty; — s.f. A little; a particular.
s.f.pl. (of juzwī), Parts; particulars.
s.f. = ajwāʼin, jawānī, qq.v.
s.m. Flame, blaze, light.
s.m. 'Flame-tongued'; a name of Agni or fire
subs: jwālā-mukhī, s.f. A volcano; any place where subterranean fire or inflammable gas breaks forth (such places being held sacred by the Hindūs as indicating the presence of a form of Durgā); a title of the goddess Devī (Durgā), and of her shrine in the district of Kāṅgrā, in the Panjāb.

def: s.m. 'Flame-tongued'; a name of Agni or fire
subs: jwālā-mukhī, s.f. A volcano; any place where subterranean fire or inflammable gas breaks forth (such places being held sacred by the Hindūs as indicating the presence of a form of Durgā); a title of the goddess Devī (Durgā), and of her shrine in the district of Kāṅgrā, in the Panjāb.

def: s.f. A large flame or blaze.
adj. & s.m. Burning, blazing, flaming; shining; — fire; caustic potash.
part. Kindled, burnt, consumed by fire; blazing, flaming.
s.m. Fever ( = jar)
subs: (jvara+ap˚), s.f. A febrifuge, the plant Medicago esculenta
(jvara+ag˚), s.m. Feverish heat, the hot paroxysm in fever
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit ज्वर (jvará).

def: adj. Febrile, feverish, afflicted with fever.
s.f. A bow-string; the chord of an are; (in Geom.) a sine; — the earth; a mother.
s.f. An elder sister; — name of the eighteenth lunar mansion; — the middle finger; — misfortune (personified as a goddess and the elder sister of Lakshmī).
adj. & s.m. Eldest-born, oldest, &c. ( = jeṭh, and jeṭhā, qq.v.)
subs: s.m. One of the first caste, a Brahman.

def: s.f. = S جييشٿتو ज्येष्ठत्व jyeshṭhatva, s.m. Superiority; primogeniture, seniority.
s.m. = S جييشٿهتا ज्येष्ठता jyeshṭhatā, s.f. Superiority; primogeniture, seniority.
s.m. (dialec.) = jīv, or jīʼū, q.v.; — adv. = jyoṅ, q.v.

ety: Prk. जेम, जिंव, जेंव; S. यथा; or यावत्
def: adv. As, like; how much; the more so; when; as if
s.f. Light, brilliance, lustre, radiance, splendour ( = jot, joti); a sunbeam; flame (of a lamp or candle); — a vision; — intelligence

ety: used chiefly in comp., and changeable to jyotish and jyotir
def: s.f. Light, brilliance, splendour, &c. ( = jyoṭi, q.v.)
subs: s.m. 'Circle of the luminaries,' the zodiac
adj. Luminous, brilliant, radiant, splendid, resplendent, glorious; heavenly, celestial
adj. Consisting of light, brilliant, abounding with stars, starry
An astrologer, one who casts nativities (syn. gaṇak)

def: s.m. Astronomy; astrology; a kind of spell
adj. & s.m. Of or relating to astronomy, or astrology; — an astronomer; an astrologer.
s.f. A star; a planet; an asterism.
s.m. An astronomer; an astrologer ( = jyotishī).
adj. & s.m. = jyotishī, q.v.
s.m. A luminary, a heavenly body; the luminaries arranged under five heads, viz. the sun, moon, planets, fixed stars, and lunar mansions (regarded by the Jains as a class of deities).
s.m. pl. (of the preceding), The sun, moon, planets, fixed stars, and lunar mansions.
s.f. Moonlight
subs: s.m. 'A friend of the moonlight,' the ćakor or Greek partridge
s.m. The tree or tripod of a lamp, a lampstand, a candlestick.

def: s.f. A moonlight night; a night in which the moon is full.
s.m. A name applied to any chief deity, or object of reverence, as Brahmā, Vishṇu, &c.; — air or wind; water; the sun; the mind, the soul; the head.
prep. (or rather, governing noun), After the similitude of, like, as
subs: adj. As though non-existent; non-existent; null and void; — kaʼl-ʻadam jānnā (-ko), To regard as non-existent; to treat as null and void; to set at nought
v.t. To make null and void, to annul, rescind, &c.
v.n. To become extinct; to come to an end; to come to nought; to become null and void, to be rescinded, &c.
As if he (or it) was, as it were
'The like of what,' like that which, like, such as, as
as (it was) in the beginning or originally; as before

As it truly is or should be; as is right or proper, aright, in a proper manner, properly; ka-mā hiya or hī, As things are, as the affair stands; — s.f. Nature or real state of a thing; — ka-mā yambagī, As is proper, &c. ( = kamā ḥaqqu-hu, q.v.).

ety: Prk. किअउं; S. कृतं, perf. part. neut. of कृ 'to do,' &c.
def: postpn. m. (f. kī; obl. & pl. ke), Of; belonging to; concerning; made of, consisting of, &c. (kā is the postpn. of the gen. case in Urdū and Hindī, and, as such, is also frequently used as an adj. termination, e.g. sone-kī ghaṛī, 'a gold watch'; kāṭh-kā ghoṛā, 'a wooden horse'; in the case of the pronouns of the first and second persons, kā takes the form rā; and in some dialects of Hindī rā is the gen. postpn. of nouns as well as pronouns; while in others lā is found in place of kā and rā; cf. also kar, karā, ker, and kerā, which are rustic forms of kā).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit किअउं (kiaüṃ), from Sanskrit कृतं (kṛtaṃ) (perfect participle neuter of कृ (kṛ, “to do”)). Dasa also suggests क (ka, diminutive suffix). Compare Hindi का (kā) and Punjabi دا (dā).

def: a Taddhita affix much used in forming adjectives; it is also added to nouns to express diminutiveness, depreciation, contempt, &c. (taking, in Persian words, the more common form of ak), and also similarity, or resemblance.

r: kā, ka, ku, ka-pūt, ku-putr, kā-purush
def: = H ك क ka, = S ك कु ku, A prefix implying uselessness, badness, deterioration, depreciation, defectiveness, &c. (e.g. ka-pūt, or ku-putr, 'a bad son'; kā-purush, 'a vile man; a coward').
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit किअउं (kiaüṃ), from Sanskrit कृतं (kṛtaṃ) (perfect participle neuter of कृ (kṛ, “to do”)). Dasa also suggests क (ka, diminutive suffix). Compare Hindi का (kā) and Punjabi دا (dā).

r: ka, ku, kā, ka-pūt, ku-putr, kā-purush
def: = S ك कु ku, = S کا का kā, A prefix implying uselessness, badness, deterioration, depreciation, defectiveness, &c. (e.g. ka-pūt, or ku-putr, 'a bad son'; kā-purush, 'a vile man; a coward').
pron. Who? which? what?; — (in Braj) the inflect. base of the oblique cases of the sing. of the interrog. pron. kā = H. kaun (and thus corresponding to H. kis).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit किअउं (kiaüṃ), from Sanskrit कृतं (kṛtaṃ) (perfect participle neuter of कृ (kṛ, “to do”)). Dasa also suggests क (ka, diminutive suffix). Compare Hindi का (kā) and Punjabi دا (dā).

def: see s.v. ك ka.
s.m. 'Space, interval'; a measure of length, the distance between the extremities and the middle of a bow
subs: s.m. Two bows' length; two cubits' length.

def: s.m. A cavern, pit; a furrow; — a cup; a large (wooden) bowl (holding as much as a man may drink at a single draught); — hidden meaning (of a speech).

ety: prob. formed from S. कियत् after the analogy of jab, and tab, qq.v.
def: adv. When? at what time? — kab-tak, or kab-talak, or kab-taʼīṅ, Till when? up to what time? how long? — kab-se, From what period? since when? how long? — kab-kā (-kī, -ke), Of what time, or period? how long since? since when? — long since, ever so long ago
subs: When; now and then, occasionally, rarely, but seldom
At what period? When? — kab-lag, or kab-lo (or-lau, or-loṅ, or-lauṅ) = kab-tak, q.v.

def: s.m. A poet.
s.m. The ankle-bone, the ankle; a heel; a geometrical cube; cube (in Arith and Alg.); a die (for playing or gaming).
s.m. The sound of a sword falling upon any object.

ety: prob. for orig. ḵẖāv; akin to Zend khawza; S. kubja; P. kūza
def: s.f. (orig.) 'A vessel, a case'; a large (china or earthenware) dish (used for serving rice, &c. in at a meal; or for kneading dough in, &c.).
s.f. A long gown with the skirt and breast open (and sometimes slits in the armpits); a (quilted) garment; a tunic.
s.m. = kaʻbat, q.v.
subs: s.m. A visitor or pilgrim to the Kaʻba.

def: s.m. Roasted meat, roast; collops, or slices, or small pieces of meat, roasted on a skewer or spit; — adj. Roasted
subs: v.t. To roast; to burn; to inflame
To put meat down to roast
v.n. To be roasted; to be scorched or burnt; to burn (with envy, or rage, or love), to be enraged; to be desperately in love
s.m. Slices or pieces of meat roasted on a skewer or a spit
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کباب (kabāb), from Arabic كَبَاب (kabāb).

def: s.f. Cubebs; (by misapplication) jungle cloves; Cassia buds.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کباب (kabāb), from Arabic كَبَاب (kabāb).

def: s.m. Cubebs, Piper cubeba.
adj. Fit to be roasted; roasting (e.g. kabābī murg̠ī, 'a roasting fowl'); s.m. One who roasts; one who sells roast meat.
s.m. A weak bow for practising.
s.f. A boys' game resembling our 'Prisoners' base' (kabaḍḍī kabaḍḍī is the cry of the boy who runs out to touch or capture an adversary).

ety: for A. قباحة, inf. n. of قبح 'to be base,' &c.
def: s.f. Baseness, base action, villainy, rascality, turpitude, deformity; perversion, abuse; crime, wrong; evil, ill; inconvenience; harm, detriment, damage, loss; imperfection, deficiency, defect, foible.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قَبَاحَت (qabāhat), from Arabic قَبَاحَة (qabāḥa). First attested in c. 1564 as Middle Hindi قباحت (qbaḥt /⁠qabāhat⁠/).

r: kabahū, kab-hū, kabhū, kabahūṅ, kabhuṅ, kabhūṅ
def: adv. = next, q.v.

r: kabahūṅ, kabhuṅ, kabhūṅ, kabahū, kab-hū, kabhū
def: adv. = next, q.v.
s.m. pl. (of qabīla, q.v.), Tribes; — family, wife and children; household.

ety: for A. قبالة qabālat, v.n. fr. قبل 'to be surety for another'
def: s.m. A contract (of bargain and sale); a deed, title-deed; bond; writing, written agreement; bill of sale; conveyance
subs: vulg. qabāle-dār, s.m. Holder of a bond, &c.

def: s.m. A big barrel or cask; a large-bellied vessel; the belly; — (met.) a cloud; — a headless trunk (esp. one retaining vitality).
s.f.(m.?) A load or bundle (of grass, or firewood, &c.); a heap or collection (of miscellaneous articles); old or broken furniture (cf. kāṭh-kabāṛ); rubbish.
s.m. and f. = kabāṛ, q.v.
adj. Gray, dirty white; spotted, mottled, speckled, &c.; — s.m. A mixed soil of clay and sand; — a spotted or speckled bird, &c.
s.m. = kibāṛ or kewāṛ, q.v.
s.m. (f. -in), A seller of old or broken furniture, &c.; a marine-store dealer.
s.m. = kabāṛī, q.v.
s.m. (f. -in), A seller of old or broken furniture, &c.; a marine-store dealer.
s.m. = kabāṛī, q.v.
s.m. Anything for kindling fire with; a match; a firebrand.
s.f. A die or play-bone; the square temple at Mecca; a square building (generally)
subs: s.f. The temple of God, the square temple of Mecca.

def: s.f. (obl. dual of kaʻbat), The two sacred temples of Mecca and Jerusalem; — two dice; a game with two dice.
s.f. The river Cavery (in the Deccan).
s.m. Baseness; shamefulness, foulness; ugliness, deformity.
adv. Sometime or other, sometimes; at any time, ever; ever so long ago
subs: (-kī, -ke), adj. & adv. Of sometime; sometime ago, long since
adv. At one time — at another time; now — now
adv. Sometimes, now and then, at times, occasionally
adv. Sometime or other, sooner or later, first or last; rarely, seldom

def: adv. Sometime or other, sometimes; at any time, ever; ever so long ago
subs: (-kī, -ke), adj. & adv. Of sometime; sometime ago, long since
adv. At one time — at another time; now — now
adv. Sometimes, now and then, at times, occasionally
adv. Sometime or other, sooner or later, first or last; rarely, seldom

def: s.m. A wise man; a poet
subs: or kabīśwar (˚vi+īś˚), s.m. The chief of the wise men; prince of poets, poet-laureate; an eminent poet (syn. malikuʼsh-shuʻarā).

def: adv. (colloq.), corr. of kabhī, q.v.
s.m. The liver; the heart.
adj. Base, vile, detestable; bad, shameful, disgraceful; deformed; ugly, ungainly.

s.m. King of the cockroaches. (It is believed that the cockroaches will spare any book in which this, the name of their king, is inscribed; and it is therefore frequently written on the first blank page of books copied in India.)

ety: act. part. of قبل 'to receive; to admit'; &c.
def: part. adj. & s.m. Receiving, taking; admitting (of, -ke), capable (of); sufficient (for); possible, practicable; liable (to); capable, able, clever, skilful, competent, fit, qualified; deserving, worthy; — a fit or competent person, an able man, &c.; — qābil-ě-adā, adj. Able to pay, solvent; — payable (a bill, &c.); due, owing; liable for payment
subs: adj. Capable of being received; payable (a bill of exchange)
(-ko), To make clever, or skilful, or capable, &c.
(-ke), To be or become fit (for); to be competent, or skilled, &c.; to be deserving (of), to deserve, merit, to be liable (to).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قابل (qābil), from Arabic قَابِل (qābil).

def: prop. n. Cain (son of Adam)
s.m. Family, race, tribe; kindred; progeny; — species, kind, class; category
subs: or min qabīl, Of the class, or kind, &c.; of the nature (of).

ety: for A. قابلة qābilat, fem. of qābil
def: s.f. A clever or skilful woman, &c.; — a midwife; — a male screw; a bolt.

ety: for A. قبيلة qabīlat, fem. of qabīl
def: s.m. Tribe, clan; family; wife

ety: for A. قابلية; qābil+abst. aff. īyat
def: s.f. Aptitude to receive, capacity, capability, fitness, suitableness; skill, ability, power; sufficiency; possibility.
s.m. Marriage-portion, settlement (on a wife), jointure, dower; — kābīn-nāma, s.m. A marriage settlement, a deed of dower.
adv. (colloq. or dialec.), corr. of kabhīṅ, q.v.

ety: act. part. of کبر 'to be large or great,' &c.
def: adj. Great, grand, illustrious (cf. kabīr).

adj. & s.m. Great, large, immense; full-grown; senior; — a nobleman, a grandee; — name of a great Hindū reformer, saint, and author who flourished about the close of the fifteenth century; — a kind of indecent song (sung by the lower classes)
s.m. = kabīr, q.v.

ety: for A. کبيرة kabīrat, fem. of kabīr
def: s.m. A great or heinous sin.
s.m. A follower of Kabīr ( = kabīr-panthī).
adj. Quick, expeditious (esp. applied to a stallion camel).
s.f. An earth with which earthenware is varnished.

ety: for A. کبيسة kabīsat, fr. کبس 'to fill up'
def: adj. Intercalary
s.m. See s.v. kabi.
s.m. See kavīśvar, and kabīsar.
adj. & s.m. Niggardly, miserly; — a niggard, a miser.
s.f. An earth with which earthenware is varnished.
s.m. See s.v. kabi.
s.m. See kavīśvar, and kabīsar.
s.m. A sort of verse in Hindī poetry; a poem, poetry, song.
s.m. A poet.
s.f. Poetry; a poem
subs: To compose verses (in Sanskrit or Hindī).

def: s.f. Poetry; a poem
subs: To compose verses (in Sanskrit or Hindī).

ety: act. part. of قبض 'to take, seize, grasp,' &c.
def: part. adj. & s.m. Taking, seizing, grasping, holding, possessing; — astringent; constipating; — taker, seizer, &c.; holder, possessor, occupier, occupant, party in possession; a sequestrator; — an astringent, a constipator
subs: s.m. 'The seizer of souls,' the angel of death
or qābiẓ ho-baithnā (-par), To seize (on), get hold (of), take possession (of); to enter on occupation (of), or settle (upon, another's land).
wety: Borrowed from Arabic قَابِض (qābiḍ).

def: s.m. A species of partridge, or quail
subs: The mountain partridge, Tetrao coturnix (i.q. kabk); the Greek partridge, or ćakor
adj. Graceful as the partridge in gait or motion.

def: s.m. The anterior or fore-part, the front; — adj. Anterior, prior, first; preliminary, preparatory; — prep. & adv. (of time), Previous (to, -ke), prior (to), before
subs: Before that; ante quam; prior to; previously, beforehand
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قبل (qabl), from Arabic قَبْل (qabl).

def: s.f. Grave, tomb
subs: s.f. Consideration or dues paid to a zamīndār or proprietor (by Muhammadans) for permission to dig a grave on ground belonging to him
'To dig up the dead from the grave'; to rake up old grie vances; to renew old quarrels
To have one foot in the grave, to be on the point of death.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قَبْر (qabr), from Arabic قَبْر (qabr).

def: adj. (f. -ī), Variegated, spotted, speckled, mottled; grey, dirty white.
s.m. Lighting, or getting a light or fire (from another); asking for fire; striking fire.

ety: said to be der. fr. Turk. qāpū, for qapū, 'a gate'; q.d. by which one may arrive at the object of one's desire
def: s.m. Opportunity; possession, hold, grasp, grip; control, authority; command, power, will
subs: (-par), To find or get an opportunity; to get into (one's) power or control, have the whip-hand (over), to be revenged (on)
(apnā), To exercise (one's) power or control (over, -par); to take advantage (of)
v.n. (One's) power or authority, &c. to go or reach; to be able to do or accomplish (syn. bas ćalnā)
v.t. To put (one) into possession (of, -kā), to effectuate possession
v.t. To keep under control or subjection, to control, restrain, &c.
or qābū-meṅ lānā, v.t. To bring under control or subjection, to subdue; to acquire an ascendency over; to bring into (one's) possession or grasp, to obtain or take possession of; to secure
s.m. (local) An astute fellow, a long-headed rascal, a crafty fellow, &c. (see qābūćī)

def: s.m. A gate-keeper, door-keeper; — adj. & s.m. Despotic, tyrannical; — selfish, interested (syn. ḵẖẉud-g̠araẓ); — wily, artful, cunning, crafty; — a selfish person, &c.; a scamp, a good-for-nothing fellow, &c.
s.m. Azure, blue; — a sort of sheepskin of an azure colour; (local) a crane; — a kind of willow.
adj. Blue, azure, cerulean.
s.f. A box or nest for pigeons, a pigeon-house (usually made of reeds, or cane, or bamboo), a dove-cot.
s.f. = kābuk, q.v.
prop. n. Kābul (or Cabul), the capital of Afgānistan (formerly a province of the Mogal empire).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کَابُل (kābul), see there for more.

ety: inf. n. of قبل 'to receive, accept,' &c.
def: s.m. Accepting; acceptance; consent, assent, compliance; concurrence; approbation; recognition, sanction; favourable reception; concession; admission, acknowledgment, confession, avowal; — adj. Accepted; approved; consented, &c.
subs: s.m. An answer (filed in a suit) acknowledging the justice of a demand
adj. 'Of approved form,' comely, handsome, beautiful

v.t. To accept, receive, take; to entertain (a petition, &c.); to assent to; consent to; to acquiesce in; to comply with, concede, grant; to recognize, sanction; to admit, acknowledge, own, confess; to approve; to make choice of; to prefer (to, -par)
v.n. To be accepted; to be agreed to, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic قُبُول (qubūl).

def: adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to Kābul; — a native, or an inhabitant, of Kābul; — s.f. A kind of pea (that came from Kābul)

r: qabūlī, qabūl, lit., chick-pea, khićaṛī, mūng
def: (fr. qabūl), s.f. lit. 'Acceptable, grateful, palatable'; a kind of dish made of rice and gram-pulse (chick-pea) boiled together (it differs from khićaṛī only in chick-pea being used in place of mūng).

ety: for A. قبولية qabūlīyat; qabul+abst. aff. īyat
def: s.f. Acceptance or hearing (of prayer by God); an acceptance; a receipt; assent, consent; approbation, liking: a written agreement (to pay rent, &c.), the counterpart of a lease.
v.t. = qabūl karnā, q.v.s.v. qabūl.
s.m. The nightmare; — modus certus coeundi.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic كَابُوس (kābūs).

def: s.m. One who rears pigeons; one who keeps sporting pigeons, a pigeon-fancier; (met.) a gallant
subs: s.m. A young pigeon; — an unripe poppy-head, wrapped up in peasemeal and fried in butter or oil
The snow-white (but inodorous) blossom of the Naga-mallie, Justicia nasuta
The Naga-mallie root, or Rhinacanthus communis.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کَبُوتَر (kabūtar, “pigeon; dove”).

def: s.f. A female pigeon; — (met.) a village, or rustic, dancing-girl.

ety: inf. n. of قبض 'to take, seize,' &c.
def: s.f. Seizing, catching; seizure, capture; taking possession of; possession; confiscation, sequestration; — contraction; astringency; constipation, costiveness; — a receipt, voucher; — tax, tribute
subs: s.f. A receipt, an acknowledgment, a discharge, an aquittance-roll
v.t. To catch, seize; to take possession of; to contract, &c.
adv. In the way of seizure, or confiscation, or distraint
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قَبْض (qabz), from Arabic قَبْض (qabḍ). First attested in c. 1657 as Middle Hindi قبض (qbẓ /⁠qabz⁠/).

ety: for A. قبضة qabẓat, fr. qabẓ, q.v.
def: s.m. Grasp, gripe (of the hand), clutch; power; possession; occupancy; holding, tenure, tenancy, tenement; — a handle, hilt (of a sword); a hinge; the upper arm
subs: (-kā), To remove the hold (of); to disturb the possession (of); to oust (from)
v.n. To obtain hold or possession (of, -par); to enter on possession
(-ko), To cause (one) to get possession (of), to put (one) in possession
(-ko), To give over possession (to), to deliver (to); to make over charge (to)
(-par), To lay hold (of), to hold fast, to clutch, grasp; to hold, keep, retain, detain; to take possession (of); to be in possession (of), to have in hand, to own, enjoy
(-par, or -ko?), To seize (on), to lay hold (of); to take possession (of); to usurp; to possess oneself (of), get hold (of), to acquire
(-ko), To bring into one's grasp, or power, or possession; to seize, &c. ( = qabẓa karnā, q.v.).

ety: for A. قبضية, abst. s. fr. qabẓī or = qabẓ + abst. aff. īyat
def: s.f. Seizure; confiscation, &c. ( = qabẓ); contraction; astringency; constipation.
s.m. Glass; crystal; an ornament of crystal; quartz; alkaline ashes; any salt of potash or soda in a glassy or crystalline state; a class of diseases of the eye (as gutta serena, &c.)
subs: s.m. Black salt, a medicinal salt (prepared by calcining fossil salt and the fruit of the emblic myrobalan together; it consists chiefly of muriate of soda, with a small quantity of iron, lime, and sulphur, and is a tonic aperient)

def: s.m. The hair (esp. of the head); — a cicatrix, a dry or healed sore, a scar; — a binding, a band; the hem (of a garment).
s.m. The water in which raw rice has been washed.
adj. (in comp.), Raw, &c. ( = kaććā); — s.m. Rawness, crudeness; inexperience; simplicity; unsoundness; weakness; — soreness, vexation, grief
subs: s.m. pl. Children, young ones, brats
s.m. An unstable person; a weak or a fickle person
v.n. To feel a soreness, to suffer vexation or grief, to be vexed, or grieved, or sorry
(prob. kaṭ-kelā = kaṭh-kelā), s.m. A kind of plantain that is eaten boiled as a vegetable
or kać-lohiyā, s.f. Iron in the ore or crude state, unwrought iron, pig iron
or lohū, s.m. Bloody ichor discharged along with purulent matter.

def: adj. (prov.) = kaććā, q.v.
s.m. (dialec.) corr. of kāćhā, or kāćh, q.v.

ety: kaććā, q.v.+āhaṭ = āwaṭ = Prk. अवट्टी = S. वृत्तिः; — and ˚āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. ता+इका
def: s.f. Rawness, &c. ( = kaććā-pan, q.v.); indigestion; surfeit
subs: (-kī), 'To take the greenness or inexperience out' (of), to beat or punish severely; — to make up a deficiency.

def: s.f. A court of justice, tribunal; a public office; a town-house; — the people assembled, or the business proceeding, in a court, or in an office
subs: s.f. The rising of a court
To close or adjourn a court; to close or dismiss an office, to cease working
v.n. To be brought into court; to go into court
adj. (f. -kī), Of or relating to a court or an office; official
v.n. To hold a court, to hear cases in a court; to carry on official work
(-kī), To collect a crowd; to make a noise or uproar
In a full court, in open court.

def: adj. Of or belonging to a court; attending or hanging about a court.

ety: kaććā, q.v.+āhaṭ = āwaṭ = Prk. अवट्टी = S. वृत्तिः; — and ˚āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. ता+इका
def: s.f. Rawness, &c. ( = kaććā-pan, q.v.); indigestion; surfeit
subs: (-kī), 'To take the greenness or inexperience out' (of), to beat or punish severely; — to make up a deficiency.

def: s.f. Grittiness ( = kić-kić).
s.f. A sudden jerk or shock; a strain, sprain; a sharp shooting pain (as of a wound, &c.); — a limp.
adj. Thick, close, densely packed, crowded ( = kaćpać, or gać-pać, gać-gać).
s.f. 'Hair against hair'; pulling each other's hair; altercation, squabble, sharp dispute, brawl.
s.f. 'Hair against hair'; pulling each other's hair; altercation, squabble, sharp dispute, brawl.

ety: kaćak˚, prob. fr. S. कष, or कर्ष+कृ; cf. kasaknā
def: v.n. To receive a jerk, or a sprain, &c.; to sprain, strain; to twist; to limp; to shoot or dart with pain.
s.m. Altercation, &c. ( = kaćākaćī).
s.m. The esculent root Arum colocasia; — a salad.
wety: From Sanskrit कचु (kaču) and आलु (ālū).

def: s.m. = kaćhār, q.v.

ety: dim. of kaćrā, q.v.; — ī = S. इका
def: s.f. Zerumbet, Curcuma zerumbet; and Cucumis Madraspatanus; — a small melon (resembling a colocynth) streaked with green and yellow, Cucumis pubescens, and C. dudaïm; — the Hedychium spicatum.

ety: dim. of kaćrā, q.v.; — ī = S. इका
def: s.f. Zerumbet, Curcuma zerumbet; and Cucumis Madraspatanus; — a small melon (resembling a colocynth) streaked with green and yellow, Cucumis pubescens, and C. dudaïm; — the Hedychium spicatum.
s.f. The sound produced by chewing (esp. fruit, India rubber, &c.).
s.f. A kind of pastry made of flour and bruised pulse, fried in ghī or oil; — a kind of dry tobacco (syn. amānat-ḵẖānī).
s.m. pl. See s.v. kać.
s.m. = kaćpaćiyā, q.v.

ety: prob. S. क ( = कु or कद्)+पचत+कः, rt. पच्
def: adj. (f. -ī), Undressed, uncooked, unboiled; unripe, raw, green; immature, crude, undigested; unfinished, imperfect, incomplete; rude, rough, sketchy; imperfectly known or acquired; imperfectly skilled or practised (person); uninstructed, inexperienced, unknowing, simple, silly; unsteady, unstable; timid, faint-hearted; of imperfect make or texture, slight, inferior; weak, frail, brittle; young, tender (understanding); — built of unbaked bricks, built of mud; — unsound, false; disturbed, broken (sleep); not fast (as colour); not permanent, temporary (an appointment); not come to a head, hard, blind (as a boil); deficient, defective, minor, smaller, less; below the standard (of weight or measure); — unauthenticated, uncertain, doubtful, vague, not to be relied on
subs: s.m. Gross proceeds, or collections, or receipts, gross income
s.m. Two-fifths of a pakkā bīghā, q.v.

adj. & s.m. Half-cooked or baked, partly dressed; half-ripe; built partly of baked bricks and partly of mud; containing both the original rough and the finally drawn out or wrought up; crude, doubtful, uncertain, not to be relied on, not established or verified; — brickwork with mud for mortar
v.t. To dress, cook, or fry partially; — to settle (a bargain)
s.m. Cotton loosely spun into thread; raw cotton, thread or yarn
s.m. A stupid person, a fool
s.m. Unvarnished story, bonâ fide account
adj. Soft-hearted, tender-hearted; timid; deficient in courage, unmanly
s.m. Paint or colouring matter unprepared by boiling; not fast colour, colour (of cloth) that will wash out
v.t. To make angry, to irritate; — to discourage; to restrain (one, from a design, &c.), to withhold or keep back; to shame, to abash

or kaććā honā, v.n. To become irritated or angry, to get into a rage; — to lose heart, to become discouraged; to restrain or withhold oneself, to forbear; to hold back (through fear, or other motive); — to be put to shame, to be abashed

A temporary cultivator, a cultivator who has no permanent or hereditary right of occupation; a farmer, or contractor, or tenant, or applicant, &c. disqualified by bankruptcy or deficient means; — a temporary post or appointment
s.m. pl. Rude or unpractised writing, an unformed hand
Clay bricks (not baked, but dried in the sun)
A dish made by boiling rice in soup, placing layers of rice and meat one on top of the other and allowing to simmer
or kaćće din, s.m. pl. The first four or five months of pregnancy
The primary hearing of a case
or kaććī āmdanī = kaććā upaj, q.v.
A road that is not metalled or macadamized
The raw or tumid itch ( = kaććā koṛh)
v.n. lit. 'To play with mud marbles'; to do anything raw, or inexperienced, or childish
lit. 'To make (one) bring water with a pitcher of unbaked clay'; to make one pass through a difficult ordeal.
wety: Inherited from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀓𑀘𑁆𑀘- (*kacca-). Compare Sanskrit काषण (kāṣaṇa, “unripe”).

def: adj. = کچا kaććā, q.v.

ety: kaććā, q.v.+pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. Unripeness, rawness, greenness; crudity, immaturity; inexperience, simplicity; inferiority, &c. (see kaććā).
s.m. A turtle, a tortoise
subs: s.m. 'Tortoise and fish'; dwellers in the water.

def: s.m. Land bordering on water; shore, bank; watery soil, marshy ground.
s.m. (f. -ī), A tortoise, a turtle; the second of the ten incarnations (avatār) of Vishṇu.
s.m. lit. 'Of or belonging to Kaććh' (on the river Indus); — a horse from the province of Kaććh (these horses are remarkable for their hollow backs).
s.m. = next, q.v.
s.m. The plant Arum colocasia.

ety: S. कच्छ+erā = Prk. इल्लओ = S. इल+कः
def: s.m. Name of a tribe.
s.f. = kaćahrī, q.v.
s.m. Side, flank (syn. bag̠al); side or corner (of a wall, &c.); one side or half (of a yoke, &c.); the buttocks, posteriors; the privities.

ety: Prk. कच्छो and कच्छओ; S. कक्षः and कक्ष+कः
def: s.m. A cloth worn round the hips, passing between the legs, and tucked in behind (syn. dhotī; langoṭī); short drawers (reaching a little way down the thighs); the upper part of the thigh; the buttocks, posteriors (cf. kāćh)
subs: v.n. To put on, or to tie up, a kāćh; to cover the privities
v.n. To wear a kāćh; (met.) to play a part
(-par), To open the kāćh; to outrage modesty; to copulate (with)
v.n. To bind on, or tie up, the kāćh ( = kāćh bīṅdhnā, and kāćhnā).

ety: Prk. कच्छो and कच्छओ; S. कक्षः and कक्ष+कः
def: s.m. A cloth worn round the hips, passing between the legs, and tucked in behind (syn. dhotī; langoṭī); short drawers (reaching a little way down the thighs); the upper part of the thigh; the buttocks, posteriors (cf. kāćh)
subs: v.n. To put on, or to tie up, a kāćh; to cover the privities
v.n. To wear a kāćh; (met.) to play a part
(-par), To open the kāćh; to outrage modesty; to copulate (with)
v.n. To bind on, or tie up, the kāćh ( = kāćh bīṅdhnā, and kāćhnā).

def: s.f. A woman of the kāćhī caste; the wife of a kāćhī or gardener; a female gardener.
s.m. Moist low land (by a river); marshy land (at the foot of a mountain); bank (of a river); alluvial formation.
s.m. = kaćhār, q.v.
s.f. An (earthen) water-pot with a wide mouth.

ety: kaćhār˚, prob. = S. प्रक्षालय(ति), rt. प्रक्षल्
def: v.t. To wash, rinse, cleanse, purify.
s.f. A cloth worn between the legs to conceal the privities (cf. langoṭī).

s.m. A caste of industrious cultivators (much employed in market and flower gardens), a caste of gardeners; an individual of that caste, a gardener (who cultivates and sells vegetables and pot-herbs).
s.m. A field or bed of vegetables; a kitchen-garden.
v.n. = kaćiyānā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Incontinent, lewd, lecherous, dissolute; — a lewd fellow, a libertine, a lecher.

ety: kāćh, q.v. + nā = lā = S. ल+कः
def: s.m. = kāćh.
v.t. To gather; to skim.
v.t. To bind on, or tie or tuck up, the kāćh or dhotī ( = kāćh bāṅdhnā or kāćh mārnā).
v.n. To be washed, to be rinsed; — s.m. Washing, &c.

ety: Prk. कच्छलओ and कच्छलिआ; S. कक्ष+ल+क and इका
def: s.f. (dim.) [Prk. कच्छलओ and कच्छलिआ; S. कक्ष+ल+क and इका], Short trousers or breeches covering very little of the thigh ( = kāćhā; syn. jāṅghiyā).

ety: Prk. कच्छलओ and कच्छलिआ; S. कक्ष+ल+क and इका
def: s.f. (dim.) = H کچهنا कछना kaćhnā, s.m. [Prk. कच्छलओ and कच्छलिआ; S. कक्ष+ल+क and इका], Short trousers or breeches covering very little of the thigh ( = kāćhā; syn. jāṅghiyā).

ety: dim. of kāćh, and f. of kāćhnā, qq.v.
def: s.f. A cloth worn over the kāćh; — also = kāćh, and kāćhnā.
pron. (old H. & Braj) = kućh, q.v.
s.m. A tortoise, a turtle; — low land
subs: adj. Uneven-bottomed (a river), deep and shallow alternately; uneven (land) with pools of water (on it); — s.f. An uneven tract of land partially flooded, or having pools of water on it.

ety: Prk. कच्छओ (with व inserted); S. कक्ष+कः
def: s.m. A side, flank ( = kaćh); one side or half (of a yoke, &c.)
subs: s.m. The proprietor or owner of a kaćhuʼā; a partner who provides half the team of cattle needed for ploughing.

def: s.f. A female tortoise (see kaćhuʼā).
s.m. & adv. A very small quantity, the very least.
m. = H کچهکي कछुकी kaćhukī, f. (dim.) = kaćhūk, q.v.
f. (dim.) = H کچهك कछुक kaćhuk, m. = kaćhūk, q.v.
s.m. The designation of a tribe of Rājpūts who claim descent from Kūs the son of Rāma-ćandra (the Rājās of Jaynagar are of this family).
s.m. A subdivision of the Kurmī tribe (cf. kāćhī).
s.m. A patch of ground cultivated by kaćhīs or gardeners; a vegetable-field.
s.f. Milk pottage.
s.m. A reaping hook, a sickle.
adj. Timid, timorous; shrinking, shy, reserved.
s.f. Timidity, dread; shrinking; shyness; aversion; disgust, abhorrence.
v.n. To be timid, to be frightened; to startle; to draw back, to shrink (from, through fear, &c.), to be averse (to, -meṅ); to be shy; to feel ashamed or abashed.

s.f. Sound from a gritty substance under the teeth; — debate, discussion, dispute, altercation, wrangle; chit-chat; chatter, clack, hubbub of voices; — adv. Grittily sounding or tasting; — with a clack or clatter, &c.
subs: (or kićkić) karnā or maćanā, v.n. To debate, to wrangle; to chatter, to clack, to make a hubbub ( = kaćkaćānā).

def: s.f. The act of debating, or wrangling, &c.; altercation, clack, &c. ( = kaćkać).

ety: prob. fr. S. कच 'a hair,' by redupl.
def: v.n. To swarm, to be very abundant; to be closely or densely packed, to be stuffed together.

v.n. To have a gritty sound under the teeth, to be gritty; — to sound as india-rubber does when being chewed; — to make a clack or clatter, to chatter; to debate; to wrangle, &c.; — v.t. To grind, gnash, or chatter (the teeth).
s.m. A jerk, shock, &c.
v.t. To jerk; to strain, sprain; to twist; to cause to shoot or dart with pain.
s.m. Tortoise-shell; — whale-bone.

ety: S. काञ्चन + कदलकः; — or kać = kaććā + kelā
def: s.m. A kind of plantain which is eaten cooked, or boiled as a vegetable.
s.f. A (beggar's) cup or bowl (usually the lower half of a broken pitcher); a wallet; — a miscellany; a common-place book; an album.
s.f. The plant Dolichos pruriens; cowhage or cowitch (syn. kawāṅć).
s.m. Clay, &c. ( = kaćrā, q.v.).
s.f. (dialec.) = keṅćlī, q.v.
s.f. See s.v. kać.
s.m. See s.v. kać.
s.m. Black salt, a medicinal salt prepared by calcining fossil salt and the fruit of the Emblic myrobalan together (it is said to promote digestion).
s.f. Prating, babble, unintelligible or incoherent language, gibberish, jargon (cf. kaj-maj).
